event_text: Mahmud of Ghazni begins a series of raids into northern India.
Mahmud of Ghazni raids the Somnath temple.
Muhammad Ghori defeats Prithviraj Chauhan at the Second Battle of Tarain.
The Delhi Sultanate begins under Qutb ud-Din Aibak.
Alauddin Khalji becomes Sultan of Delhi.
The Tughlaq dynasty takes power in Delhi.
The Vijayanagara Empire is founded in southern India.
The Bahmani Sultanate is founded in the Deccan.
Timur sacks Delhi.
Vasco da Gama reaches Calicut, opening a sea route from Europe to India.
Babur defeats Ibrahim Lodi at Panipat and founds the Mughal Empire.
Akbar becomes Mughal emperor.
The Vijayanagara Empire is defeated at Talikota.
The English East India Company is chartered.
Jahangir becomes Mughal emperor.
Construction begins on the Taj Mahal.
Aurangzeb becomes Mughal emperor.
Shivaji is crowned and strengthens the Maratha state.
Aurangzeb dies and Mughal authority begins to weaken.
Nader Shah sacks Delhi.
The East India Company wins the Battle of Plassey.
Company forces win the Battle of Buxar.
The Regulating Act increases British parliamentary oversight of the East India Company.
Tipu Sultan is killed at Seringapatam.
The Third Anglo-Maratha War ends Maratha power and expands Company rule.
Sati is banned in Company territories.
Macaulay’s Minute influences English education policy in India.
India’s first passenger railway runs between Bombay and Thane.
The Indian Rebellion begins at Meerut and challenges Company rule.
The British Crown takes direct control of India from the East India Company.
Queen Victoria is proclaimed Empress of India.
The Indian National Congress is founded.
The partition of Bengal fuels nationalist protest.
The All-India Muslim League is founded.
The capital of British India is moved from Calcutta to Delhi.
India enters the First World War as part of the British Empire.
The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurs in Amritsar.
The Indian National Congress approves Gandhi’s Non-Cooperation Movement.
Gandhi leads the Salt March.
The Government of India Act expands provincial autonomy.
The Quit India Movement demands an end to British rule.
The Bengal famine causes massive loss of life.
India becomes independent and is partitioned into India and Pakistan.
The Constitution of India comes into force and India becomes a republic.
India begins its First Five-Year Plan.
The States Reorganisation Act redraws state boundaries mainly along linguistic lines.
India fights a border war with China.
India and Pakistan fight the Indo-Pakistani War of 1965.
India and Pakistan fight a war that leads to the creation of Bangladesh.
India conducts its first nuclear test, code-named Smiling Buddha.
Prime Minister Indira Gandhi declares the Emergency, suspending many civil liberties.
Operation Blue Star is followed by Indira Gandhi’s assassination and anti-Sikh violence.
Economic liberalisation reforms begin after a balance-of-payments crisis.
The Babri Masjid is demolished in Ayodhya.
India conducts the Pokhran-II nuclear tests.
The Kargil War is fought between India and Pakistan.
A major earthquake strikes Gujarat.
The Indian Ocean tsunami affects India’s eastern and southern coasts.
Coordinated terrorist attacks strike Mumbai.
The Mars Orbiter Mission is launched.
Narendra Modi becomes prime minister.
India demonetises ₹500 and ₹1000 banknotes.
The constitutional status of Jammu and Kashmir is changed.
The COVID-19 pandemic causes major public health and economic disruption in India.
Chandrayaan-3 lands near the Moon’s south pole region.
